name: lion-demons
shortDescription: The lion-demons were monstrous offspring of Tiamat, created to wage war against the younger gods in Babylonian mythology.
description: The lion-demons were among the first generation of monsters spawned by Tiamat, the primordial goddess of salt water and chaos, and her consort Apsu. These creatures were part of Tiamat's army, created out of her rage after the death of Apsu at the hands of the younger gods. They were fierce and terrifying beings, combining the strength and ferocity of lions with demonic features, embodying the raw, untamed power of the primordial chaos from which they originated. They fought alongside other monstrous creations like scorpion men and fish-men in Tiamat's war to avenge Apsu and restore the old order. Their creation and involvement in the war against the younger gods is a key part of the Babylonian creation myth, highlighting the struggle between order and chaos. Ultimately, they were defeated by Marduk, who used his divine powers and weapons to vanquish Tiamat and her monstrous army, establishing order and creating the world from her remains. The lion-demons represent the chaotic forces that had to be overcome for civilization to emerge in the Babylonian worldview.

sources.title: Myths from Mesopotamia: Creation, The Flood, Gilgamesh, and Others
sources.author: Stephanie Dalley

sources.title: The Religion of Babylonia and Assyria
sources.author: Theophilus G. Pinches
